profile.title: Jenkin
profile.introduction: With a high Crit. Rate and Crit. Damage, Brown and the swarm of rats are her key allies in fighting multiple enemies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
{Mice Assemble} consumes Arithmetica to generate {Swarm Impact}. The more Arithmetica consumed, the higher the number of hits, allowing for high DMG by saving Arithmetica.
Every time you play "{Get 'em! Brown!}," both {Get 'em! Brown!} and {Swarm Impact} deal increased Damage.
Exalt and {Rouse} can increase Jenkin's Crit. Rate and Crit. DMG, further enhancing her DMG output.
In this stage, bring a level 50 Jenkin with Skill level 5 and Enlighten 3, receiving 1 {Insight} per turn, while the enemy is strengthened on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Everyone quickly accepted a fact: where there's Jenkin, there's Brown, and where there's Brown, there's Jenkin. And if Brown acts alone, don't doubt it—Jenkin is behind it.
"What do you mean I stole it? I have an alibi!"
The girl said, holding the item "taken" by Brown.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 315, @2, @1, 08:13 a.m..
The third team of the committee's on-site response and members of the Dissolution Item Control and Management (DISM) β7 group arrived at Corroded Area L-E-933 (formerly 2 Southwark Street, Rogers Wax Museum) to take over the task from the first team and DISM α2 group, conducting area neutralization work.
09:24 a.m..
DISM member James experienced symptoms of headache, nausea, and dizziness while clearing the wax museum's underground area.
10:47 a.m..
Third team member Roland, DISM members Zachary and Bolton fled the underground area. They reported being engulfed by darkness into a starry void, where tangible colors intertwined, both beautiful and chilling. These colors then formed countless mice that attacked them.
Bolton refused to continue and returned to the committee's Londinium office. Third team member Roland and DISM Zachary accompanied third team leader Fisher back into the underground area for further inspection.
13:03 a.m..
After a search, third team leader Fisher found and recovered Silver L3202 from the remaining corroded wax in the underground area. It has been handed over to Mythag University for further processing.
—<Italic:"Neutralization Report of Corroded Area L-E-933">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Jenkin's nimble hands once made people worry she'd become a regular feature in the Mythag University Daily—not as an interviewee, but as a prime suspect in thefts.
Though she did become a regular, it wasn't due to old habits—just enough cupcakes can make even a street survival expert enjoy a peaceful retirement with her rat buddy.
The real reason she frequented the paper? The editor paid too well.
Shh—keep it down, you never know where she might be, ready to trade info with Hannah for more sweets and cakes

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
"I'm not an orphan," said six-year-old Jenkin. "My mom just went to work. She'll pick me up after she's done."
She never wavered, nor did she dare to. This belief was her protective charm, waking her from nightmares filled with black slime. It was her only comfort whenever she tried to recall her mother's face but could only see a black shadow.
Through spring, summer, autumn, and winter, she stood by the welfare home's gate. Only on the morning the home disbanded did she wipe away her invisible tears and leave with her mouse friend.
"I am an orphan," said ten-year-old Jenkin. "I don't have parents. I only have Brown."
She told this to herself and the mouse perched on her shoulder. It was true—people might be wary of children suddenly approaching; East End street children were infamous throughout Londinium—but no one suspected mice scurrying by their feet. Through this tacit cooperation, the girl occasionally enjoyed a full meal and dreamed of fireplaces and pastries in the sewers.
But the cold winter spared no one, bringing illness and high fever, with death standing resolutely beside her. The girl lay dying, while mice scurried anxiously around her, hoping for any attention at all.
A young woman stopped and wrapped the girl warmly in her embrace.
"I'm not an orphan," said thirteen-year-old Jenkin. "I have my sister Mason and my good friend Brown!"
The girl stood before an old house, holding the mouse close, her face bright with a radiant smile.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Street life is not difficult for her.
When hungry, she looks for work; if there are no jobs, she makes a living with her own hands. Her coordination with Brown is so tacit that they can sustain a life of food and drink without anyone else's help.
"We don't need anyone," she swore with her mouse partner, "we only need each other." 
But every snowy night, she cannot help but glance at those windows lit with dim yellow lights, watching those who embrace each other by the meager dinner, blessing one another.
She truly does not need a concealed roof, does not require luxurious high beds and soft pillows; she indeed has trustworthy partners, but she is still curious what it would feel like to have a home for a long time?
This question was answered when Mason adopted her.
